content.text: I’ll research this as a read-only, official-source-only pass, focusing on R2 lifecycle limits, object metadata mutability, and Worker/cache enforcement.

content.text: Raw official-search result: R2 lifecycle expiration is a destructive delete action (typically within 24 hours); Workers `head()` returns metadata without the body; and Worker caching can cache a 200 for two hours by default if headers are omitted. I’m narrowing the metadata-update constraint now.

input.input:
const r = await tools.web__run({"search_query":[{"q":"site:developers.cloudflare.com/r2 \"metadata can only be updated\" R2"},{"q":"site:developers.cloudflare.com/r2 \"metadata\" \"CopyObject\" \"REPLACE\""},{"q":"site:developers.cloudflare.com/r2 \"customMetadata\" \"put\" overwrite object"},{"q":"site:developers.cloudflare.com/r2 \"R2Object\" \"metadata\" \"immutable\""}],"response_length":"long"});
text(JSON.stringify(r));

const r = await tools.web__run({"search_query":[{"q":"site:developers.cloudflare.com/r2/buckets/public-buckets custom domains public access objects Worker access only"},{"q":"site:developers.cloudflare.com/r2/api/s3/presigned-urls expiration direct R2 bypass Worker"}],"response_length":"medium"});
text(JSON.stringify(r));

content.text:
Recommendation: use an immutable `customMetadata.expiresAt` set at upload (`now + 14 days` unless explicitly overridden), and allow every GET/HEAD only through a Worker that reads and enforces it. Return `410 Gone` (or `404` if existence should be concealed) after expiry; do not configure R2 lifecycle expiration.

- R2 lifecycle rules are unsuitable: their expiry action deletes objects, usually within 24 hours of the expiration value. They cannot make an object logically unavailable while retaining it. [Object lifecycles](https://developers.cloudflare.com/r2/buckets/object-lifecycles/)

- `customMetadata` is the durable policy field: R2 exposes it as `Record<string,string>`, supports it on `put()`, and `head(key)` returns object metadata without fetching its body. For GET, use one `get()` and evaluate its metadata before streaming; for HEAD, use `head()`. Metadata is limited to 8,192 bytes total. [Workers R2 API](https://developers.cloudflare.com/r2/api/workers/workers-api-reference/), [R2 error limits](https://developers.cloudflare.com/r2/api/error-codes/)

- `httpMetadata` is for emitted HTTP headers (`writeHttpMetadata(headers)`), including cache-related headers; it does not enforce authorization/expiry. It can complement the response with a matching `Expires`/`Cache-Control`, but `customMetadata.expiresAt` should remain the authoritative policy value. [Workers R2 API](https://developers.cloudflare.com/r2/api/workers/workers-api-reference/)

- Avoid later metadata mutation where possible. The Workers binding documents `put()` as storing both value and metadata and exposes no metadata-only patch. The S3-compatible `CopyObject` supports `MERGE`/`REPLACE` metadata directives, but this is a destination write and has documented non-atomicity caveats; fixed-at-upload expiry avoids it. [R2 S3 extensions](https://developers.cloudflare.com/r2/api/s3/extensions/)

- Cache is a correctness boundary: return `Cache-Control: no-store` for the simplest strict guarantee, including expired responses. Without it, Workers caching may store a successful response and serve later range requests without invoking the Worker; Cloudflare says `no-store` bypasses cache and runs the Worker every request. If caching is required, cap TTL to the remaining lifetime and use `must-revalidate`; do not use stale-while-revalidate beyond expiry. [Workers cache configuration](https://developers.cloudflare.com/workers/cache/configuration/)

Uncertainties: this only protects reads routed through the Worker. Keep the bucket private and disable any `r2.dev` or direct R2 custom-domain access; public bucket endpoints serve objects directly and bypass this logic. [Public buckets](https://developers.cloudflare.com/r2/buckets/public-buckets/)

Recommended next action: implement a Worker-only download route that writes `expiresAt` on upload, treats missing metadata as `object.uploaded + 14 days` for migration compatibility, and returns `410` plus `Cache-Control: no-store` once expired.
